enforced.oracle: Toggling RNEXT between the explicit RNAME and the '=' alias must produce a semantically identical SAM file. The spec explicitly defines '=' as a synonym for 'same as RNAME', so both forms are equivalent.

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR/SEQ length mismatch must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on a record where sum of query-consuming CIGAR ops (M,I,S,=,X) does not equal len(SEQ); silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: Flag-bit exclusivity violation (unmapped flag on mapped record) must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when FLAG 0x4 is set on a record with RNAME != '*' and POS > 0; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when two reads of a paired template carry same-signed TLEN values (both positive or both negative) instead of opposite signs; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

enforced.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: When FLAG bit 0x1 (multiple segments) is unset, clearing mate-context bits (0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80) must produce a semantically equivalent record. SAMv1 §1.4 states these bits are only meaningful when 0x1 is set.

evidence.quote: If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle @CO comment lines in SAM header
quarantine.oracle: The SAM file after shuffling @CO comment lines must be semantically equivalent to the original. Parsing, validation, and data extraction must produce identical results.

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format ` TAG:VALUE' where TAG is a two-character string that defines the format and content of VALUE. Thus header lines match /94@(HD|SQ|RG|PG)(92t[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]:[ -126]+)+$/ or /94@CO92t.*/.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @HD header line
quarantine.oracle: The SAM file after shuffling @HD subtags must be semantically equivalent to the original. Parsing must produce identical alignment data.

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @SQ header lines
quarantine.oracle: The SAM file after shuffling @SQ subtags must be semantically equivalent to the original. Parsing must produce identical alignment data.

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @RG header lines
quarantine.oracle: The SAM file after shuffling @RG subtags must be semantically equivalent to the original. Parsing must produce identical alignment data.

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @PG header lines
quarantine.oracle: The SAM file after shuffling @PG subtags must be semantically equivalent to the original. Parsing must produce identical alignment data.

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Permute optional tag fields on SAM alignment records
quarantine.oracle: The SAM file after permuting optional tag order on alignment records must be semantically equivalent to the original. All data values must be preserved.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Reorder @SQ header records while preserving @HD first
quarantine.oracle: The SAM file after reordering @SQ lines must be semantically equivalent to the original. The @HD line must remain first.

evidence.quote: @HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: The @SQ order defines the coordinate sort order per SAMv1 §1.3, so reordering @SQ lines changes coordinate sort semantics for files sorted by coordinate. This MR is valid only for unsorted or queryname-sorted files, or when the oracle accounts for the changed reference ordering.

quarantine.oracle: After splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations, the alignment semantics (reference consumed length, query consumed length, and alignment position) remain unchanged; the file should parse identically and produce the same biological interpretation.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY severity — 'should' not 'must'; some parsers may not enforce this

quarantine.oracle: Removing zero-length CIGAR ops and merging newly-adjacent identical ops preserves the alignment semantics; the file should parse identically before and after.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY severity — 'should' not 'must'; zero-length ops are not explicitly forbidden by the spec

quarantine.oracle: Uppercasing the SEQ field preserves the biological sequence information; the file should parse identically since the SAM spec permits both upper and lower case letters in SEQ.

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Character set restrictions] Thus they match the following regular expression:

ambiguity_flags: The spec regex permits both cases but some parsers may treat case differently in downstream processing

quarantine.mr_name: Illegal optional tag type character must be rejected
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when an optional tag's TYPE character is not one of the valid set {A,i,f,Z,H,B}; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: In an optional field, TYPE is a single case-sensitive letter which defines the format of VALUE

A & [!-126] & Printable character \\ i & [-+]?[0-9]+ & Signed integer\\ f & [-+]?[0-9]*92.?[0-9]+([eE][-+]?[0-9]+)? & Single-precision floating number \\ Z & [\,\,\,!-126]* & Printable string, including space\\ H & ([0-9A-F][0-9A-F])* & Byte array in the Hex format\\ B & [cCsSiIf](,[-+]?[0-9]*92.?[0-9]+([eE][-+]?[0-9]+)?)* & Integer or numeric array

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record MAPQ normalization round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: When FLAG bit 0x4 (unmapped) is set, normalizing MAPQ to 255 must produce a semantically equivalent record. SAMv1 §1.4 states MAPQ 255 indicates mapping quality is not available.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

ambiguity_flags: MAPQ 255 is defined as 'not available' but some tools may preserve the original MAPQ value even for unmapped reads.

quarantine.mr_name: Header VN version bump round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: Toggling @HD VN between SAM versions 1.5 and 1.6 must produce a semantically equivalent file since the changes between these versions are backwards-compatible clarifications.

evidence.quote: This specification is for version 1.6 of the SAM and BAM formats. Each SAM and BAM file may optionally specify the version being used via the @HD VN tag.

ambiguity_flags: Some parsers may reject files with a VN they don't recognize or may change behavior based on the version string.
